prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `e84fef15`.
codex_response:
Implemented `e84fef15` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e84fef15/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e84fef15/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e84fef15/generator.py#L4). The rule is: remove the color-`3` separator frontiers, split the remaining `25x25` area into `25` `5x5` tiles, take the most common tile as the reference, and replace with `1` every cell where the single odd tile differs. The summary hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it; no `notes.md` or new shared primitive was needed.

The generator stays on-family by fixing the `29x29` layout, using full `3` separators, making a sparse `5x5` motif over dominant color `8` with colors from `{0,2,4,6}`, and corrupting one repeated tile by turning `2-4` motif cells back to `8`. Official verification passed `4/4`, the shared build produced `1000` examples, and generated-example verification passed `1000/1000`. I also spot-checked representative built examples from the generated task data to confirm the expected separator layout and sparse motif distribution.
